topic.what: Privacy-preserving federated log anomaly detection using tiny LLMs with LoRA and differential privacy
topic.how: Combines FedProx aggregation with DP-SGD using Rényi accounting across 14-15 clients training 1.1-1.5B parameter models via LoRA adapters
topic.why_matters: Enables multi-organization collaborative anomaly detection without centralized data sharing while providing formal privacy guarantees

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification accuracy for normal vs anomalous log windows
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs centralized LogTinyLLM: -0.035 to -0.059 accuracy gap
characteristics.evidence: Table 14: FlogTinyLLM vs LogTinyLLM on BGL; Table 17: Thunderbird comparisons
characteristics.context: BGL: 0.9324-0.9569 across 4 models; Thunderbird: 0.9825-0.9876 across 4 models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Harmonic mean of precision and recall
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs centralized LogTinyLLM: -0.13 to -0.21 F1 gap on BGL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of true positives among positive predictions
characteristics.vs_baseline: Exceeds Federated LogBERT (0.7574) and Federated LogGPT (0.9211) on BGL

characteristics.what_itasures: Proportion of actual anomalies correctly identified
characteristics.vs_baseline: Lower than Federated LogBERT (0.9625) and LogDeep(FL) (0.8611) on BGL
Relaxed DP guarantee for utility-privacy tradeoff
characteristics.context: Central DP with Rényi accounting across 20 rounds

characteristics.what_it_measures: Wall-clock time for federated training over 20 rounds
characteristics.vs_baseline: 3-50x slower than baseline federated models (0.39-3.42 hours)
characteristics.context: OPT-1.3B fastest at 14.2h; TinyLlama slowest at 43.9h on BGL

characteristics.vs_baseline: Enables 0.44% trainable parameters vs full model

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of federated clients in experiment
characteristics.context: Round-robin data distribution by compute node

characteristics.what_it_measures: Area under ROC curve for anomaly detection
